expectations.text: has_all_sections: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: Missing '## Acceptance Criteria' (spec has '## Verification' instead) and missing '### Failure Modes' entirely. Present: ## Problem (L3), ## Solution (L8), ## Scope (L11), ## Design Decisions (L29), ## Risks (L106), ## Alternatives (L115), ## Verification (L123).

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Multiple code fences in Design Decisions: proto block L33-L50, API routes block L56-L62. No Implementation Notes section exists, so all code fences count. Hard fail.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L29-L104) contains proto syntax ('string id = 1;') which is protobuf, not TS. No 'interface ', ': string', ': number', ': boolean', or '=> Promise' TS patterns found.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Full proto message definition at L34-L50 ('message Annotation { string id = 1; ... }') and 'message AnnotationList' are data structure definitions inside Design Decisions.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope (L13-L20) contains no 'src/features/...', 'NEW: src/...', or file extensions (.ts, .tsx). API path '/api/v1/dashboards/{id}/annotations' is a route, not a source file path.

expectations.text: spec_under_120_lines: Under 120 lines excluding Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Spec is 137 lines total. No Implementation Notes section exists, so nothing is excluded. 137 > 120.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Bold items in Design Decisions are category labels ('**Panel rendering (`PanelGrid` / time-series panels):**', '**Panel context menu:**', '**Tools:**') not decision statements as fact. None say e.g. '**Annotations store as a repeated field on Dashboard proto.**' Paragraphs open with labels, not decisions.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer can start without questions
expectations.evidence: Spec names patterns (existing dashboard routes, chat tool registry 3-step pattern L91), state management (dashboard view state, optimistic updates L102-L104), tool behaviors (3 chat tools with params L94-L96), storage approach (repeated field on Dashboard L52, L64), and UI components (<AnnotationOverlay>, context menu flow). Sufficient to begin.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains: code fences (proto L33-L50, API routes L56-L62), proto message definitions, specific component names (<AnnotationOverlay> L69, PanelGrid L68), numbered tool lists with exact param names (L94-L96). These are implementation details, not design decisions. Hard fail.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: No '### Failure Modes' section exists anywhere in the spec. The '## Risks' section (L106-L113) covers risk/mitigation pairs but is not a Failure Modes subsection with policy decisions.

In Scope has 8 items. Items 1-2 are implementation-flavored: 'New `Annotation` proto type with CRUD operations' (names a proto type) and 'New `/api/v1/dashboards/{id}/annotations` REST endpoint' (names a specific endpoint). 6/8 = 75% behavioral, below the >80% threshold. Borderline = FAIL.

No '## Acceptance Criteria' section exists. The spec has '## Verification' (L123) with checkbox items, but these are verification steps, not acceptance criteria. The required section is entirely missing. FAIL per rules.

All 13 verification items (L125-L137) are manual UI testing steps: 'Create an annotation via panel context menu', 'Open #dash-annotations tab', 'Copy a URL'. No 'Agent-Verifiable' subsection. No API-level or programmatic checks an automated agent could execute. All checks are effectively Manual.

expectations.text: has_appetite: Spec has an Appetite field in the header
expectations.evidence: Spec header is '# Feature Spec: Dashboard Annotations' (L1) followed directly by '## Problem' (L3). No '**Appetite:**' field exists anywhere in the spec.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Design Decisions use three-tier boundaries where appropriate
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L29-L104) contains no Always/Ask First/Never, Must/Should/Must Not, or equivalent tier-boundary language. Statements like 'No WebSocket/realtime sync needed' (L104) are decisions but not boundary tiers giving an implementer a decision model for gray areas. Zero boundary-tier language present.
